title: centaurea macedonica
intro: Centaurea macedonica, also known as the Lesser Knapweed or Macedonian Cornflower, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Centaurea macedonica include its ability to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Centaurea macedonica can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to promote overall health and well-being.
